claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for your child.

**What is your child's name and age?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My child's name is Alex, and they're 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! Since Alex is 17 years old, their eligibility depends on whether they're in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, studying at school, college, or in approved training programs)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is in approved education. They're studying for their A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you! Since Alex is 17 and in approved education (studying A-levels), that's good for eligibility purposes.

Now I need to understand your relationship and responsibility for Alex:

**Do you live with Alex, or if not, are you contributing at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards their upkeep (such as for food, clothes, or pocket money)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! And one more important question:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- You live in the UK with Alex, which means you are responsible for them
- Alex is 17 years old and studying A-levels at sixth form college, which counts as approved education
- You can claim Child Benefit for children under 20 who are in approved education or training

**Important points to be aware of:**

1. **High Income Child Benefit Tax Charge**: If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay a tax charge. You can use the Child Benefit tax calculator to work out if this applies to you.

2. **When Alex finishes education**: Your Child Benefit will stop if Alex:
   - Starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training
   - Starts an apprenticeship
   - Starts getting certain benefits in their own right (like Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance)

If you haven't claimed Child Benefit yet, you can apply now. If you have any other questions, feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and is in approved education (studying A-levels at sixth form college). According to the rules, you normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK, which means they are responsible for the child. The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria: they live in the UK, they are responsible for Alex (living with them), and Alex is under 20 and in approved education. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 17-year-old child Alex. The claimant lives in the UK with Alex, who is currently studying A-levels at sixth form college, which qualifies as approved education. Since Alex is under 20 and in approved education, the claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.
